spec_name: NCERT Physics Class XI Part I — Extraction Rules
provenance: Every rule traces to a measured defect or verified invariant in the source PDFs. Validation constraints derived from ACTUAL failures in the chemistry extraction (extracted/lech101/): truncated titles, empty section text, exercise numbers 0.1/273.15/2.3/120.7 parsed from question prose.

preflight.note: Run before any parsing. Each corresponds to a measured defect.

steps.name: repair_pua_text
steps.description: Undo Private-Use-Area font mapping. MUST run before any regex.
steps.code: ''.join(chr(ord(c)-0xF000) if 0xF000<=ord(c)<=0xF0FF else c for c in s)
steps.without_it: keph107 (10/17 pages) and keph1ps (13/16 pages) yield garbage

steps.name: derive_column_split_per_page
steps.description: Cluster block x0 values per page; never hard-code.
steps.algorithm: left = min(x0); right = first x0 > left+200; split = (left+right)/2
steps.without_it: hard-coding x=283 works on keph101 odd pages and silently corrupts every even page and all of keph107

steps.name: determine_parity_from_geometry
steps.description: Infer parity from measured x0 or printed page number in running header.
steps.reason: wrong for keph107 whose PDF page 1 uses even margins

patterns.pattern: chapter title alone on a line at y<75

deliberately_absent.reason: Physics has none - verified across all 7 chapters. A field that is always empty is a liability; omit it or pin it to 0.

target_schema.naming_note: use 'answer' NOT 'solution' - physics uses the Answer keyword

sections.detect_by: line-level match on size + colour, NOT font name

two_color_trap.description: headers are TWO-COLOUR: the number is BLACK #231F20, the title is CYAN #00AEEF
two_color_trap.wrong_approach: filtering spans by color==0x00AEEF matches the title only and silently drops the number
two_color_trap.right_approach: detect the LINE, then read number and title from separate spans

sections.font_varies: 1.3.2 uses Bookman,Bold; 1.6.1 uses Bookman-Demi for the same role. Match on size+colour, NEVER font name.

title_rejoining.problem: titles wrap across lines; raw text gives '1.2 The international system of' / 'units'
title_rejoining.rule: while next line is same-font/same-colour and current title has no terminal punctuation, append it

sections.authoritative_source: the page-1 sidebar TOC is authoritative for section TITLES only

toc_omits_subsections.warning: the TOC lists sections only (1.1-1.6), never subsections
toc_omits_subsections.evidence: chapter 1's 1.6.2 'Deducing Relation among the Physical Quantities' is in the body but absent from the TOC
toc_omits_subsections.rule: subsections MUST be discovered from in-body headers; trusting the TOC alone reports 0 subsections

examples.note: question text is the ONLY body text in the book coloured #00BDF2
warnings: do NOT search for 'Solution' - physics uses 'Answer'
do NOT assume the answer is inside the box - it is not

extraction_method: pdfimages will MISS these - they are vector
2. locate figure bbox via caption position + vector drawing cluster

tables.colon_note: physics omits it ('Table 1.1 SI Base Quantities and Units*'); chemistry includes it ('Table 1.2: Values of...')

breakout_blocks.note: all three are full-width breakouts - disable two-column logic once detected

points_to_ponder.heading: POINTS TO PONDER
points_to_ponder.on_absence: treat as null, NOT as a parse failure

killer_bug.description: values inside question text match this pattern
killer_bug.evidence: chemistry's extraction produced exercise numbers 0.1, 273.15, 2.3, 120.7 because it regexed blindly

guards_all_three_required.name: expect_next_number_only
guards_all_three_required.rule: track expected = (chapter, last_n+1); reject any match that isn't it

guards_all_three_required.name: colour_check
guards_all_three_required.rule: exercise numbers are cyan bold #00AEEF; numbers in prose are #231F20

killer_bug.reference_code:
def is_exercise_number(m, chapter, last_n, span_color):
    return (int(m[1]) == chapter and int(m[2]) == last_n + 1 and span_color == 0x00AEEF)

answers.missing_reason: descriptive questions with no numeric answer

validation.policy: fail loudly - a silent bad extraction is worse than a crash

hard_constraints.rule: exercise numbers form exactly {c}.1..{c}.max, contiguous, no gaps, no duplicates
every exercise number has chapter == c
no section title ends with a preposition/article (of, the, in, a, and, for, with)
set(answers) is a SUBSET of set(exercises)
hard_constraints.rationale: answers are partial; subset, not equality

hard_constraints.rule: no text contains PUA chars U+F000-U+F0FF
no text contains 'not to be republished' / 'Reprint 20'

soft_constraints.rule: points_to_ponder present for ch 2-7, null for ch 1
section count matches sidebar TOC length

ground_truth_fixtures.note: measured from source, not assumed - assert against these

6.title: Systems of Particles and Rotational Motion

regression_test_order.note: extract in this sequence - it surfaces bugs earliest

order.why: clean baseline, no PtP, no PUA
WORST CASE: PUA (10 pg) + CropBox (0,0) + parity anomaly

regression_test_order.principle: If keph107 passes, the pipeline is sound. Testing only keph101 proves almost nothing - it is the single least representative chapter in the book.

recommended_pipeline.principle: tiered by concern - do not solve everything with one tool

tiers.tool: PyMuPDF get_text('dict') + fix_pua + per-page column split

recommended_pipeline.why_not_pdftotext: it discards colour and font, which are the ONLY reliable separators for examples (#00BDF2 vs #231F20) and exercise numbers (cyan vs black). It also mangles two-column reading order. Use PyMuPDF dict mode.
recommended_pipeline.where_vision_earns_its_cost: not bulk text - use it to spot-check pages that fail validation, and to read figure content. Running vision on all 184 pages is ~$2 and unnecessary if V1-V10 pass.
